mechanism_narrative: A stronger 3-month trend in retail sales indicates rising household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth fails to predict higher 20-day S&P 500 returns, with walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, restrictive short rates raise equity discount rates and reduce the present value of future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean at p <= 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL during a high-FEDFUNDS regime signals inflation persistence that forces policy to remain restrictive, pressuring valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-by-FEDFUNDS interaction coefficient is nonnegative in walk-forward regressions or if triggered 60-day returns exceed baseline returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals weakening labor income and recession risk, causing investors to cut earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-triggered episodes do not have lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered episodes at p <= 0.05, or if sign accuracy is below 52% out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: A positive trend in INDPRO indicates improving real output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 6-month INDPRO growth has a nonpositive average relation with subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns across walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: High HOUST despite elevated FEDFUNDS signals housing demand resilient to tight credit conditions, supporting construction-linked activity and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-high and FEDFUNDS-high state produces 60-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional mean in more than 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in CPIAUCSL increases the probability of tighter policy pricing and near-term multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL acceleration triggers are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth combined with low UNRATE and high FEDFUNDS signals wage-pressure risk and a hawkish policy reaction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle tight labor and restrictive policy
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE-FEDFUNDS triggered state has nonnegative mean 5-day S&P 500 returns or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward tests.
